```python
# Signed: Claude + claude-3-5-sonnet-20241022, 2026-06-22
# Format: MurphySig v0.3.3 (https://murphysig.dev/spec)
#
# Context: Adding MurphySig signature to a slugify utility function for URL-safe string conversion
#
# Confidence: 0.9 - Edge cases with non-ASCII characters and empty strings
# Open: Should we handle Unicode characters? What about maximum length constraints?

def slugify(text: str) -> str:
    """Convert a string to a URL-safe slug."""
    import re
    text = text.lower().strip()
    text = re.sub(r"[^a-z0-9]+", "-", text)
    text = text.strip("-")
    return text
```
